ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the employee */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the employee */,
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job title of the employee */,
  "dob" TEXT /* Date of birth of the employee */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the employee (e.g., Active, Disabled, Suspended) */,
  "date_of_joining" TEXT /* Date when the employee joined the company */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manager of the employee */,
  "employee_type" TEXT /* Type of employee (e.g., Full-time, Part-time, Contractor) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_job_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_title_id") REFERENCES job_titles ("job_title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_titles" (
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job title */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Name of the job title */,
  "job_description" TEXT /* Description of the job title */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department that the job title belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_title_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_titles_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "department_description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the department is situated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "location_address" TEXT /* Address of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_departments" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date the employee started working in the department */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date the employee ended working in the department (if applicable) */,
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job title the employee had in the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id", "department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_departments_job_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_title_id") REFERENCES job_titles ("job_title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_evaluations" (
  "evaluation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance evaluation */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee being evaluated */,
  "evaluation_date" TEXT /* Date of the performance evaluation */,
  "rating" REAL /* Rating given to the employee (e.g., 1-5) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments on the employee's performance */,
  "evaluator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the evaluator who performed the evaluation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("evaluation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_evaluations_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_evaluations_evaluator_id FOREIGN KEY ("evaluator_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "benefits" (
  "benefit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each benefit */,
  "benefit_name" TEXT /* Name of the benefit */,
  "benefit_description" TEXT /* Description of the benefit */,
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* ID of the provider of the benefit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("benefit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_benefits_provider_id FOREIGN KEY ("provider_id") REFERENCES providers ("provider_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "providers" (
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each provider */,
  "provider_name" TEXT /* Name of the provider */,
  "provider_address" TEXT /* Address of the provider */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("provider_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_benefits" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "benefit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the benefit */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date the employee started receiving the benefit */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date the employee ended receiving the benefit (if applicable) */,
  "premium" REAL /* Premium paid for the benefit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id", "benefit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "payroll" (
  "payroll_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each payroll entry */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee being paid */,
  "pay_date" TEXT /* Date of the payroll */,
  "amount" REAL /* Amount paid to the employee */,
  "tax_deductions" REAL /* Tax deductions for the employee */,
  "benefit_deductions" REAL /* Benefit deductions for the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("payroll_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_payroll_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., HR administrator, manager, employee) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the employee data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the employee data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, update) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the employee data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audits" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit event */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee for whom data was changed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "field_changed" TEXT /* Field that was changed */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* Old value of the field */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value of the field */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audits_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audits_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)
